title: acantholimon lycaonicum subsp. lycaonicum
intro: Acantholimon lycaonicum subsp. lycaonicum, also known as Turkish Thyme or Lycaonicum Acantholimon, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Acantholimon lycaonicum subsp. lycaonicum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for use in traditional medicine.
